task_objective: Isolate Syncthing BrowserView activity and transfer direction to the Syncthing folder containing Ludusavi's configured backup path while preserving relevant-peer connectivity handling and the frontend RPC contract.
tests_added: Connection byte totals cannot create downloading, uploading, or ACTIVE_TRANSFER after a watched-folder sequence/index mutation.

Unrelated-folder RemoteDownloadProgress, StateChanged, FolderSummary, FolderScanProgress, ItemStarted, ItemFinished, LocalChangeDetected, and LocalIndexUpdated events do not mutate watched-folder activity.
DownloadProgress entries for another folder neither start nor clear watched-folder downloads; watched-folder entries start them and the final empty payload clears them.
Watched-folder DownloadProgress and RemoteDownloadProgress preserve download and upload direction for a peer shared with another folder.
A watcher tick ignores high and changing connection totals plus another folder's event while retaining positive watched-folder progress behavior.
An idle frontend sample with no update, upload, or download evidence publishes no transfer status.

red_tests.result: Expected RED: test_connection_rates_cannot_create_transfer_direction_from_folder_mutation failed because status.downloading was True and status was ACTIVE_TRANSFER; 29 other tests passed. The subset also triggered the repository-wide coverage threshold, as expected for a partial run.
red_tests.watcher_result: Expected RED: the serialized sample reported downloading=True from aggregate connection traffic.

design_decisions: ConnectionSnapshot now carries only connected device IDs. Global and per-device connection byte counters, rate state, rate computation, thresholds, aggregate transfer fields, and compatibility exports were removed.
SyncthingWatch retains the last known connected-device set when the connections endpoint fails and still terminates when no configured peer for the resolved backup folder is connected.

Downloading is derived only from watched-folder syncing state, watched-folder DownloadProgress, or watched-folder active items. Uploading is derived only from non-expired watched-folder RemoteDownloadProgress.
Scan, local change, local index, sequence change, need counters, item-finished grace, preparing states, and error states still affect update and settlement without inventing transfer direction.
The serialized SyncthingActivitySample fields and frontend production code remain unchanged.

results.implementation: Connection polling is connectivity-only, separate-folder traffic is isolated even for a shared peer, watched-folder direction remains functional, and relevant-peer behavior is preserved.
results.release: No version, tag, packaging workflow, or release change.
results.deferred_work: On-device Steam Deck verification is deferred until an environment with two Syncthing folders sharing a peer, Decky, and Game Mode is available. Automated mocked API and event coverage is complete.

validation.result: Passed: 259 Vitest tests, frontend typecheck/build, Ruff check/format, ty, 675 pytest tests, 86.96% coverage, and review-note deletion check.
